title: The honest audience
summary: A retired first violinist plays on a street corner in Prague. A young music student finally asks him why.

body.speaker: Tomáš
body.text: Excuse me, sir.

body.speaker: Mr Holub
body.text: Yes?

body.speaker: Tomáš
body.text: I have listened to you for one year. Every day after my class.

body.speaker: Mr Holub
body.text: I have noticed you. The young man with the green bag.

body.speaker: Tomáš
body.text: You noticed?

body.speaker: Mr Holub
body.text: I notice everybody. It is part of the work. So — you are a music student?

body.speaker: Tomáš
body.text: How did you know?

body.speaker: Mr Holub
body.text: You wait for the difficult pieces. People who do not study music walk away.

body.speaker: Tomáš
body.text: I have a question.

body.speaker: Mr Holub
body.text: Please.

body.speaker: Tomáš
body.text: Why are you in the street? You play so well. You should be in a hall.

body.speaker: Mr Holub
body.text: I was in a hall. Forty years. National Theatre. Second violin, then first.

body.speaker: Tomáš
body.text: I do not understand.

body.speaker: Mr Holub
body.text: I retired. They gave me a small pension. I came outside.

body.speaker: Tomáš
body.text: But why outside?

body.speaker: Mr Holub
body.text: Because in the hall, people pay first and listen later. They are afraid to leave. They clap because the others clap. Here, no one has to stay. If they stop, they like it. If they walk, they do not.

body.speaker: Tomáš
body.text: You only see the people who like it.

body.speaker: Mr Holub
body.text: I only see the truth. In the hall, I never knew.

body.speaker: Tomáš
body.text: Do you ever miss the hall?

body.speaker: Mr Holub
body.text: Some days. The worst day here is better than the best day there. Now — listen. I will play a piece you have not heard. Stand here.

body.speaker: Tomáš
body.text: Yes, sir.

grammarNotes.title: First conditional: If + present, will / present
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu điều kiện loại 1 nói về kết quả có khả năng xảy ra ở hiện tại / tương lai. Cấu trúc: 'If + chủ ngữ + động từ ở hiện tại đơn, kết quả với hiện tại đơn (hoặc 'will + V').' Trong bài, ông Holub mô tả khán giả ngoài đường: 'If they stop, they like it. If they walk, they do not.' Đây là kết quả có thật, không phải giả định xa.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: First conditional describes a real, likely result. Form: 'If + subject + present simple, [present simple OR 'will + verb'].' Mr Holub uses it to read his audience: 'If they stop, they like it. If they walk, they do not.' The result is real, not imaginary.

translationVi:
Tomáš: Xin lỗi bác.
Ông Holub: Sao cháu?
Tomáš: Một năm rồi cháu nghe bác. Ngày nào sau giờ học cháu cũng đứng đây.
Ông Holub: Bác có để ý. Cậu trẻ đeo cái túi xanh lá.
Tomáš: Bác để ý ạ?
Ông Holub: Bác để ý tất cả. Đó là một phần công việc. Cháu là sinh viên nhạc à?
Tomáš: Sao bác biết?
Ông Holub: Cháu đợi các bài khó. Người không học nhạc thường bỏ đi.
Tomáš: Cháu có một câu hỏi.
Ông Holub: Cháu hỏi đi.
Tomáš: Sao bác lại ngoài đường? Bác đàn quá hay. Bác phải ở trong khán phòng chứ.
Ông Holub: Bác từng ở khán phòng. Bốn mươi năm. Nhà hát Quốc gia. Vĩ cầm hai, rồi vĩ cầm một.
Tomáš: Cháu không hiểu.
Ông Holub: Bác về hưu. Họ cho bác chút lương hưu. Bác ra ngoài.
Tomáš: Nhưng sao lại ra ngoài?
Ông Holub: Vì trong khán phòng, người ta trả tiền trước, nghe sau. Họ sợ rời chỗ. Họ vỗ tay vì người khác vỗ tay. Ngoài này, không ai phải ở lại. Nếu họ dừng, họ thích. Nếu họ đi, họ không thích.
Tomáš: Bác chỉ thấy những người thích.
Ông Holub: Bác chỉ thấy sự thật. Trong khán phòng, bác không bao giờ biết.
Tomáš: Bác có nhớ khán phòng không?
Ông Holub: Có vài ngày. Ngày tệ nhất ngoài này còn hơn ngày tốt nhất trong kia. Giờ — nghe đi. Bác sẽ chơi một bản cháu chưa nghe. Đứng đây nhé.
Tomáš: Vâng bác.

questions.prompt: How long has Tomáš been listening to Mr Holub?
questions.explanation: 'I have listened to you for one year.'

questions.prompt: How does Mr Holub remember Tomáš?
options: As the young man with the green bag
As the boy with the red hat
As a tourist with a camera
questions.explanation: 'The young man with the green bag.'

questions.prompt: How did Mr Holub know Tomáš is a music student?
options: Tomáš waits for the difficult pieces, and people who do not study music walk away.
questions.explanation: 'You wait for the difficult pieces. People who do not study music walk away.'

questions.prompt: Where did Mr Holub work before?
options: At the National Theatre — second violin, then first — for forty years
He has always played in the street.
questions.explanation: 'I was in a hall. Forty years. National Theatre. Second violin, then first.'
questions.hint: Mr Holub names the theatre and the years.

questions.prompt: Why did Mr Holub leave the hall?
options: He retired and was given a small pension.
questions.explanation: 'I retired. They gave me a small pension. I came outside.'
questions.hint: His reply to 'I do not understand.'

questions.prompt: What does Mr Holub say about people in a concert hall?
options: They listen more carefully than in the street.
They pay first and listen later, are afraid to leave, and clap because the others clap.
questions.explanation: Mr Holub gives all three observations.
questions.hint: Mr Holub's longer answer about why he plays outside.

questions.prompt: What can he tell about his street audience?
options: Nothing — they all look the same.
If they stop, they like it; if they walk, they do not.
questions.explanation: 'If they stop, they like it. If they walk, they do not.'

questions.prompt: Tomáš says: 'You only see the people who like it.' How does Mr Holub answer?
options: He says: 'I only see the truth. In the hall, I never knew.'
questions.explanation: 'I only see the truth. In the hall, I never knew.'
questions.hint: Mr Holub's direct reply to that line.

questions.prompt: Does Mr Holub miss the hall?
options: Some days — but the worst day in the street is better than the best day in the hall.
questions.explanation: 'Some days. The worst day here is better than the best day there.'
questions.hint: Look at his answer to 'Do you ever miss the hall?'

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the dialogue?
options: Knowing how an audience really feels can matter more than getting their applause.
Old people should retire to the street.
Music students should not study indoors.
questions.explanation: Mr Holub chose honest feedback over the comfort of guaranteed applause.
questions.hint: Think about what 'truth' means to him here.

cloze.template:
Tomáš: Excuse me, sir.
Mr Holub: Yes?
Tomáš: I have listened to you for one {{b1}}. Every day after my class.
Mr Holub: I have noticed you. The young man with the {{b2}} bag.
Tomáš: You noticed?
Mr Holub: I notice everybody. It is part of the work. So — you are a music {{b3}}?
Tomáš: How did you know?
Mr Holub: You wait for the difficult pieces. People who do not study music walk away.
Tomáš: I have a question.
Mr Holub: Please.
Tomáš: Why are you in the street? You play so well. You should be in a {{b4}}.
Mr Holub: I was in a hall. Forty years. National Theatre. Second violin, then first.
Tomáš: I do not understand.
Mr Holub: I retired. They gave me a small {{b5}}. I came outside.
Tomáš: But why outside?
Mr Holub: Because in the hall, people pay first and listen later. They are afraid to {{b6}}. They clap because the others clap. Here, no one has to stay. If they {{b7}}, they like it. If they walk, they do not.
Tomáš: You only see the people who like it.
Mr Holub: I only see the {{b8}}. In the hall, I never knew.
Tomáš: Do you ever miss the hall?
Mr Holub: Some days. The worst day here is better than the {{b9}} day there. Now — listen. I will play a piece you have not heard. Stand here.
Tomáš: Yes, {{b10}}.

blanks.explanation: Tomáš says he should be in a concert hall.
They gave him a small pension.
They are afraid to leave the hall.
If they stop, they like it.
Worst day here vs. best day there.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mr Holub gave up guaranteed applause for honest feedback. Is there a part of your life where the easy approval — from family, from a manager, from a class — might hide what people actually think? Would you give up the applause to know?
